nodes.title: OpenSpec: paused-state-surfacing
nodes.description: Execution tracker for openspec/changes/paused-state-surfacing. Surfaces the openspec:paused label visually across three UI surfaces: TaskListItem badge, KpiBar chip, and OpenSpecPanel banner.

nodes.title: 1.1: Create parsePausedNote helper
nodes.description: openspec/changes/paused-state-surfacing/tasks.md §1.1. Create `src/lib/parsePausedNote.ts` exporting `parsePausedNote(notes: string | null): string | null` — scans lines for the last `/^Paused:\s*(.+)$/` match, returns the trimmed capture or `null`.

nodes.title: 1.2: Create findScopeChangeChild helper
nodes.description: openspec/changes/paused-state-surfacing/tasks.md §1.2. Create `src/lib/findScopeChangeChild.ts` exporting `findScopeChangeChild(currentTask: Task, allTasks: Task[], blockingIds: string[]): Task | null` — checks Signal A (`Resolves:` note prefix) then Signal B (shared `openspec:<change>` label + `blocks` dependency).

nodes.title: 1.3: Vitest unit tests for parsePausedNote
nodes.description: openspec/changes/paused-state-surfacing/tasks.md §1.3. Add Vitest unit tests for `parsePausedNote`: null input, no match, single match, multi-match (last wins), trailing whitespace, lowercase `paused:` (no match), colon with no text (null).

nodes.title: 1.4: Vitest unit tests for findScopeChangeChild
nodes.description: openspec/changes/paused-state-surfacing/tasks.md §1.4. Add Vitest unit tests for `findScopeChangeChild`: Signal A present, Signal A absent + Signal B present, neither signal (null), multiple Signal B candidates (most recent wins), sibling with wrong `openspec:` label excluded.

nodes.title: 5.1: Confirm Task.notes in useTasks selector
nodes.description: openspec/changes/paused-state-surfacing/tasks.md §5.1. Inspect the TanStack Query selector used by `useTasks` (or equivalent) and confirm `notes` is included on the `Task` type in `src/bindings.ts`; if the selector strips it, update the selector to retain `notes`.

nodes.title: 5.2: Confirm Task.labels is string[] on frontend type
nodes.description: openspec/changes/paused-state-surfacing/tasks.md §5.2. Confirm `Task.labels` is already a `string[]` field on the frontend `Task` type; if it is `string | null` or missing, update the selector / bindings mapping.

nodes.title: 5.3: Add useTaskBlockers hook in OpenSpecPanel
nodes.description: openspec/changes/paused-state-surfacing/tasks.md §5.3. In `OpenSpecPanel` (or its parent `TaskDetailPanel`), add a `useTaskBlockers(task.id)` call — either reusing the result of the existing `get_task_dependencies` query or adding a narrow hook — to supply `blockingIds` to `findScopeChangeChild`. No new Rust command required; `get_task_dependencies` already returns the dependency rows.

nodes.title: 2.1: Add STATUS_BADGE_PAUSED constant in TaskListItem
nodes.description: openspec/changes/paused-state-surfacing/tasks.md §2.1. Add `STATUS_BADGE_PAUSED` constant in `src/components/task-list/TaskListItem.tsx` with the `bg-violet-900/40 text-violet-300 border border-violet-800/40` palette.

nodes.title: 2.2: Render pause pill in TaskListItem badge path
nodes.description: openspec/changes/paused-state-surfacing/tasks.md §2.2. In the badge render path, add a branch: when `task.labels.includes('openspec:paused')`, render `STATUS_BADGE_PAUSED` with text `"⏸ paused"` and a `title` attribute set to the parsed pause reason; otherwise render the existing `STATUS_BADGE[task.status]` unchanged.

nodes.title: 2.3: Confirm status dot remains driven by task.status
nodes.description: openspec/changes/paused-state-surfacing/tasks.md §2.3. Confirm the status dot (leading coloured circle) remains driven by `task.status` — no change to the dot logic.

nodes.title: 2.4: Render tests for TaskListItem pause pill
nodes.description: openspec/changes/paused-state-surfacing/tasks.md §2.4. Add a render test (Vitest + React Testing Library or snapshot): task with `openspec:paused` label renders pause pill; task without the label renders normal badge; tooltip attribute matches parsed reason.

nodes.title: 3.1: Compute pausedCount in KpiBar
nodes.description: openspec/changes/paused-state-surfacing/tasks.md §3.1. In `src/components/filters/KpiBar.tsx`, compute `pausedCount` as `allTasks.filter(t => t.labels.includes('openspec:paused')).length`.

nodes.title: 3.2: Render Paused chip after STATUS_CONFIG.map output
nodes.description: openspec/changes/paused-state-surfacing/tasks.md §3.2. Render a thin divider and a `⏸ N Paused` chip after the `STATUS_CONFIG.map(...)` output, gated on `pausedCount > 0 || state.filters.labels?.includes('openspec:paused')`.

nodes.title: 3.3: Implement click handler to toggle openspec:paused filter
nodes.description: openspec/changes/paused-state-surfacing/tasks.md §3.3. Implement click handler: toggle `openspec:paused` in `state.filters.labels` (add if absent, remove if present); apply the same `ring-1 ring-violet-500` active treatment used by other chips.

nodes.title: 3.4: Unit tests for KpiBar Paused chip
nodes.description: openspec/changes/paused-state-surfacing/tasks.md §3.4. Add unit tests: zero paused + no active filter → chip hidden; non-zero paused → chip visible with count; filter active with zero paused → chip visible; click adds label to filter; second click removes label.

nodes.title: 4.1: Add PausedBanner sub-component in OpenSpecPanel
nodes.description: openspec/changes/paused-state-surfacing/tasks.md §4.1. Add `<PausedBanner>` sub-component in `src/components/task-detail/OpenSpecPanel.tsx` (inline or extracted) that accepts `task: Task` and `scopeChangeChild: Task | null`.

nodes.title: 4.2: Render banner as first child of OpenSpecPanelBody
nodes.description: openspec/changes/paused-state-surfacing/tasks.md §4.2. Render the banner as the first child of `OpenSpecPanelBody`, before `DriftWarning`, conditioned on `task.labels.includes('openspec:paused')`.

nodes.title: 4.3: Display parsed reason with fallback in PausedBanner
nodes.description: openspec/changes/paused-state-surfacing/tasks.md §4.3. Display the parsed reason from `parsePausedNote(task.notes)`, falling back to `"(no reason recorded)"` when null.

nodes.title: 4.4: Render Resolves child button when scopeChangeChild non-null
nodes.description: openspec/changes/paused-state-surfacing/tasks.md §4.4. When `scopeChangeChild` is non-null, render a `Resolves: <child.id> — <child.title>` button that calls `setState({ view: 'all', taskId: child.id })`.

nodes.title: 4.5: Render muted note when scopeChangeChild is null
nodes.description: openspec/changes/paused-state-surfacing/tasks.md §4.5. When `scopeChangeChild` is null, render a muted `"No scope-change child detected yet"` note.

nodes.title: 4.6: Render tests for PausedBanner
nodes.description: openspec/changes/paused-state-surfacing/tasks.md §4.6. Add render tests: paused + reason + child → banner with reason + link; paused + no reason + child → fallback copy + link; paused + no child → muted note; not paused → no banner.

nodes.title: 6.1: bun run typecheck passes with no new errors
nodes.description: openspec/changes/paused-state-surfacing/tasks.md §6.1. `bun run typecheck` (`tsc --noEmit`) passes with no new errors.

nodes.title: 6.2: bun test passes — all new unit and render tests green
nodes.description: openspec/changes/paused-state-surfacing/tasks.md §6.2. `bun test` passes — all new unit and render tests green.

nodes.title: 6.3: bun run lint passes — no new ESLint warnings
nodes.description: openspec/changes/paused-state-surfacing/tasks.md §6.3. `bun run lint` passes — no new ESLint warnings.

nodes.title: 6.4: Manual smoke test — three UI surfaces show pause state
nodes.description: openspec/changes/paused-state-surfacing/tasks.md §6.4. Manual smoke test: pause an issue via `openspec-beads-scope-change`, reload the UI, confirm (a) pause pill appears on the task row, (b) KPI bar shows `⏸ 1 Paused`, (c) opening the task's panel shows the banner with the reason. Resume the issue (`bd untag <id> openspec:paused`), confirm all three surfaces clear.

nodes.title: 6.5: Manual — scope-change child Resolves link navigates correctly
nodes.description: openspec/changes/paused-state-surfacing/tasks.md §6.5. Manual: create a scope-change child issue with `bd link <paused-id> <child-id>`, confirm the banner shows the `Resolves:` link and clicking it navigates to the child.

nodes.title: 6.6: openspec validate paused-state-surfacing PASS
nodes.description: openspec/changes/paused-state-surfacing/tasks.md §6.6. `openspec validate paused-state-surfacing` PASS.
